---
title: "Day 5: AI in the Physical World"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
    center-title-slide: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [default, ../../theme/dark.scss]
    logo: ../../theme/codercub.jpg
    highlight-style: dracula
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-gradient: "linear-gradient(135deg, #0a0e1a 0%, #0d2137 60%, #1a1f35 100%)"

---

## Recap

- Learned about the micro:bit!
- Connected, recorded data, trained a model, and tested a series of actions
- Learned about the importance of diverse data
- Used MakeCode to play audio on an ML action
- Downloaded the project to the micro:bit

# Project Time!

## Project Time!

- Work in pairs again (same or different)
- Challenge: Design one of the following mini-apps
- An hour to work on it
- 30-45 minutes to show it to the rest of the class

# What Will You Create?

## Option 1: micro:bit Fitness Tracker

- Train the micro:bit to detect walking, jumping jacks, push ups, and other exercises
- Record the movements as variables
- Display "LED rewards" when goals are met (e.g., 10 jumping jacks)
- Use the buttons to reset the tracker

## Option 2: Virtual micro:bit Piano

- Train the micro:bit to detect seven different movements - different hand or body movements
- For each unique movement, play a note (A, B, C, D, E, F, or G) and display that note on the LED
- Can you make this accurate enough to play a tune?

## Option 3: AI micro:bit remote control lamp

- Train one micro:bit to detect hand movements such as hand up/down/circles/clapping
- Send the actions from the wearable micro:bit via the radio to a second powered micro:bit
- When the second micro:bit receives messages, it turns on/off the lamp (LEDs) and adjusts the brightness
- (This is a more advanced project)

## Or Your Own Idea!

- You can also create your own project, if you'd prefer
- Rules:
  - Must train the micro:bit using a minimum of three movements
  - Need to use a combination of three different features - e.g., LEDs, sound, button
  - And at least one variable in Python!

## Let's Go!!!

- Get back into pairs (same or different)
- Choose which option to make
  - (Open the slides again on your own machine to see each option again)
- Work on your project until 10.15am (1 hour)
- Project presentations
- If you get stuck, ask for help (and sample code!)

# micro:bit CreateAI Project Presentations!

![](../04/images/microbit-front.png)

## End of Week 1!

- Please disconnect and leave the micro:bits on the table
  - Don't worry about disconnecting the battery
- Tomorrow = Computer Vision!
  - Face detection
  - Object detection
  - Pose landmarkers
- A fitness tracker using a webcam
